Package nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions

Class HelpAction

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions.HelpAction
All Implemented Interfaces:
EventListener, javafx.event.EventHandler<javafx.event.ActionEvent>
public class HelpAction extends Object implements javafx.event.EventHandler<javafx.event.ActionEvent>
Handles the action of displaying help content in a JavaFX application. This class implements EventHandler for ActionEvent to trigger the display of help information, typically loaded from an HTML file into a WebView. It requires an owner Stage to properly manage the help window, ensuring the help dialog is appropriately parented.

  • Constructor Details

    • HelpAction

      public HelpAction(javafx.stage.Stage ownerStage)
      Constructs a new HelpAction.
      Parameters:
      ownerStage - The primary Stage of the JavaFX application, which will serve as the owner for the help window. This parameter must not be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if ownerStage is null.

  • Method Details

    • handle

      public void handle(javafx.event.ActionEvent event)

      Handles the action event, typically triggered by selecting a "Help" menu item or button. This method creates and displays a new Stage containing a WebView that loads and renders the help content from an HTML file. The help file is expected to be located at the classpath resource path "/help/help.html". If the help file cannot be found or loaded, an error Alert is displayed to the user.

      Specified by:
      handle in interface javafx.event.EventHandler<javafx.event.ActionEvent>
      Parameters:
      event - The ActionEvent that triggered this handler (e.g., a menu item click).

    • isHeadlessEnvironment

      protected boolean isHeadlessEnvironment()
      Allows subclasses (such as tests) to override the headless detection logic.
      Returns:
      true, if is headless environment

    • createWebView

      protected javafx.scene.web.WebView createWebView()
      Factory method used to create WebView instances. Subclasses may override this method in tests to simulate WebView failures.
      Returns:
      the web view
